claim: NVIDIA Parakeet v2 achieves better word error rates than OpenAI Whisper for English-language speech recognition
actionable: For English-only speech-to-text tasks, benchmark Parakeet v2 against Whisper; it may offer better accuracy and faster inference

claim: Parakeet v2 generates accurate word-level timestamps and predicts punctuation/capitalization natively, unlike base Whisper
actionable: If you need timestamped, punctuated transcripts, use Parakeet v2 instead of Whisper + external punctuation restoration

claim: Parakeet v2 is English-only; Whisper remains better for multilingual speech recognition needs
actionable: For multilingual applications, continue using Whisper or wait for multilingual Parakeet versions

claim: A custom chunking script enables Parakeet v2 to transcribe long audio files (26+ minutes) without shape/memory errors
actionable: When using Parakeet v2 on long audio, implement segmentation logic to handle files larger than model's context window

claim: Parakeet v2 transcribes 26 minutes of audio in ~25 seconds, enabling fast bulk transcription pipelines
actionable: For bulk podcast/video transcription, benchmark Parakeet v2 for throughput and cost per hour of audio

claim: Diarization (speaker identification) requires external models even with Parakeet v2; multimodal LLMs can infer speakers from voice differences and name mentions
actionable: For multi-speaker transcripts, combine Parakeet v2 with an external diarization tool or pass transcript + speaker list to a multimodal LLM for speaker identification
